content:
Secondly, proper spelling shows that you have a good command of the language, and it reflects positively on your level of education and professionalism. It also helps avoid confusion between words that sound alike but have different meanings, like your vs. you're or there vs. their.

Furthermore, proper grammar in college writing respects the reader and the subject matter being discussed. Poor grammar can distract readers from focusing on your ideas as they try to decipher what you mean.

Proper grammar, punctuation, and spelling also help understanding, which could lead to misinterpretation of the paper's information.

Therefore, before submitting any written assignment, please pay careful attention while proofreading to represent your intended message accurately.



After you finish this page, click Next to continue to Common Mistakes Made with Grammar, Punctuation, and Spelling.
